evt_014.label: Drop
evt_014.basis: Sponsor G was reported to be no longer participating in the process, with no filing text indicating target exclusion or a valuation comparison.
evt_014.source_text: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process.

evt_006.label: Informal
evt_006.basis: The proposal used indicative price ranges and preliminary language.

evt_007.label: Informal
evt_007.basis: The proposal used indicative price ranges and preliminary language.

evt_012.label: Formal
evt_012.basis: The proposal included a revised draft merger agreement and committed financing documentation.

evt_013.label: Informal
evt_013.basis: The proposal used an indicative price range and stated that more diligence was needed for a more definitive proposal.

evt_015.label: Formal
evt_015.basis: The proposal arrived after the July 2 formal-round request and after the July 11 offer deadline, with no extracted informal signals.

evt_016.label: Formal
evt_016.basis: Hudson's Bay said it was prepared to offer $16 per share subject to a definitive, binding agreement.

cycle_1.basis: First formal proposal in cycle: evt_012 included a revised draft merger agreement and committed financing documentation.

initiation_judgment.basis: The sale process was triggered by unsolicited bidder outreach, even though the board had been reviewing strategic alternatives generally.
initiation_judgment.source_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks' Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

advisor_goldman_sachs.source_text: Goldman Sachs, one of Saks' longstanding financial advisors, has participated in these reviews.

advisor_wachtell_lipton.source_text: Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, referred to as Wachtell Lipton, Saks' external counsel.

advisor_morgan_stanley.source_text: Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (a long-time advisor to Saks, referred to as "Morgan Stanley")

count_reconciliation.assertion: During the go shop process, Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties.
count_reconciliation.note: The filing counts unnamed go-shop contacts in aggregate; the verified extract individualizes only Company I.

count_reconciliation.assertion: Of those contacted, only six parties expressed interest.
count_reconciliation.note: The filing counts unnamed interested parties in aggregate; the verified extract individualizes only Company I.

count_reconciliation.assertion: Only one of the six (which we refer to as Company I) executed a confidentiality agreement with, and conducted a due diligence investigation of, Saks.
